data.title: Efficacy and Safety of Prostaglandin Analogue and Carbonic Anhydrase Inhibitor for the Treatment of Pediatric Glaucoma
data.brief_summary:
Objectives:

The study will assess the ocular hypotensive effect of latanoprost and dorzolamide in a selected sample of patients affected by primary Pediatric Glaucoma (PG), refractory to surgical procedures. Safety will be assessed, too.In the first version of the protocol 96 eyes were forecasted to complete the enrolment. The protocol was then amended and now to complete the study 68 eyes should be included. This number of eyes could be achieved by recruiting from 34 to 68 patients due to not
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Children of either sexes, aged 0-12 years
* Diagnosis of mono- or bilateral primary congenital glaucoma
* IOP greater than or equal to 22 mmHg and lower than 27 mmHg
* Only one previous ocular hypotensive surgical procedure for treatment of glaucoma among the following: goniot
